title: classroom50/assignments/v1
description: Machine-readable schema for <classroom>/assignments.json, mirroring the authoritative Go validators in cli/gh-teacher (assignments_json.go, tests.go, runtime.go). Intended for non-CLI clients (e.g. the GUI) to validate before writing. Two rules JSON Schema cannot express: (1) test names must be UNIQUE within an assignment, and (2) name length is capped at 100 BYTES (UTF-8), not characters — enforce both client-side. One normalization the CLI's parser applies that this schema rejects: a missing or empty `autograder` (normalized to "default") — clients should always write the normalized form, as the CLI does.

assignment.description: One assignment record. additionalProperties is true so a top-level field a newer binary or the web GUI adds survives a read-modify-write verbatim (the 'tolerate AND preserve' rule: CLI via AssignmentEntry.Extra, web via the `...source` spread). Known sub-objects (template/due_meta/runtime, and each tests item) stay closed so a typo inside one is still a hard error.

due.description: RFC 3339 timestamp. The CLI normalizes the teacher's input to a UTC instant (e.g. 2026-09-16T03:59:00Z) and records the original value/zone in `due_meta`; clients should write UTC too. The pattern still permits any offset so older files validate. Advisory: collect-scores compares it against each submission's datetime and marks rows `late` in scores.json; nothing enforces the deadline.

input.description: The due value as supplied (--due flag or migrated source deadline), whitespace-trimmed but otherwise verbatim, before UTC normalization.

zone.description: Best-effort IANA/local zone name; present only when the offset was auto-detected (an explicit offset carries no zone name).

offset.description: Zone offset applied at normalization, e.g. -04:00 (UTC is +00:00).

source.description: How the offset was determined: from the input itself, auto-detected from the machine's local zone, or carried in from a migrated deadline.

due_meta.description: Write-side provenance for `due`, for auditing a wrong-zone deadline. Advisory metadata only -- collect-scores reads `due`, never this.

mode.description: `individual` = one repo per student. `group` = a shared repo: the first accepter creates it and becomes its admin, then adds teammates with `gh student invite`. Group size is advisory — `max_group_size` is a coordination hint the founder is expected to respect, NOT a hard security cap. `gh student invite` does apply it as a best-effort guardrail (it refuses to add a collaborator past the cap when run from inside the founder's own group repo), but it is bypassable (the GitHub UI, or simply not running the CLI) — the joiner-driven `gh student group join` that once enforced it was removed. Group mode still REQUIRES `max_group_size` (>= 2) as a contract field for clients like the GUI; individual mode must omit it. See the conditional at the assignment level.

autograder.description: Workflow-shim name; `default` for the universal shim. NOT the grading logic — that's autograder.py or `tests`.

max_group_size.description: Advisory maximum collaborators on a group repo. REQUIRED for `mode: group` (>= 2) and MUST be omitted for `mode: individual`. This is a CONTRACT field, not a hard security ceiling: `gh student invite` enforces it as a best-effort guardrail (refusing to add a collaborator past the cap when run from inside the founder's own group repo), but the check is advisory and bypassable (e.g. adding collaborators via the GitHub UI) — the joiner-side enforcement in `gh student group join` was removed. The founder coordinates actual group size when adding teammates with `gh student invite`. Retained in the schema/assignments.json so clients such as the GUI can display and (optionally) enforce it client-side.

tests.description: Declarative tests. Mutually exclusive with a per-assignment autograder.py at <classroom>/autograders/<slug>/ — probe before writing.

feedback_pr.description: Opt the assignment into the Feedback Pull Request: the autograde runner opens one long-lived PR per student repo (base = a frozen branch at the baseline commit, head = the default branch) for inline instructor review of the full starter→submission diff. Product default is ON (both `gh teacher assignment add` and the GUI create it enabled). On the wire it is collapsed: the CLI omits the field when false (omitempty), so readers must treat an absent field as `false`; the GUI may also write `false` explicitly, so accept both. Absent or false means the PR never opens.

allowed_files.description: Ordered .gitignore-style patterns defining which files belong to the submission (order matters, last match wins, `!` re-includes). Example: ["*", "!hello.py"] ignores everything then re-includes hello.py — an allowlist of exactly hello.py. Empty/absent means every file is allowed. The autograde runner enforces it best-effort by removing disallowed working-tree files before grading (control files like .classroom50.yaml and .github/ are always kept), but fails open on git/resource failure — so it is a grading-scope/hygiene tool, not a secret-hiding boundary; `gh student submit` applies it as a best-effort pre-push filter.

pass_threshold.description: Opt-in, advisory passing bar: the integer percentage of max score (0–100) at/above which a gradebook client (e.g. the GUI) shows a submission as passing, plus passing/failing rollups, badges, and filters. ABSENT means the feature is OFF — no passing concept — which is distinct from a 0% threshold; clients must not treat absent as a default. CLI-unenforced like max_group_size: the autograder and score collection never read it; it changes no student's actual score. Written only when a teacher enables it.

runtime.description: Omit entirely for the defaults (ubuntu-latest + Python 3.12). Never write empty-string fields. CLOSED CONTRACT: additionalProperties is false and every reader decodes runtime strictly (the CLI's RuntimeRef has no Extra / DisallowUnknownFields; the web rebuilds runtime from the known sub-keys and drops any unknown one on a read-modify-write edit). An unknown sub-key makes the whole file unparseable, so — unlike top-level entry keys, which evolve additively and preserve unknowns — a NEW runtime sub-key must ship in lockstep across this schema, the Go RuntimeRef, and the web Assignment['runtime'] type in the same release; otherwise an older reader silently strips it on edit.

runs-on.description: GitHub Actions runner selection, mirroring Actions' own `runs-on`: a single label string ("ubuntu-latest") or an array of labels (["self-hosted", "gpu"]) for custom / self-hosted runners. No value allow-list — a teacher customizing runs-on owns getting the label right; each label is only anti-injection-checked (1-10 labels). Omit for the default (ubuntu-latest).

name.description: Unique within the assignment (enforce client-side); <= 100 UTF-8 bytes; no control characters.

timeout.description: Seconds, 1-600. 0 (or omitted) means the default of 10s.

points.description: Defaults to 0 (informational test) when omitted; the CLI always writes it.
